title: Logs, Events, Metrics, Inspect, Debugging, and Incident Evidence · Docker Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares fresh logs, events, metrics, inspect, debugging, and incident evidence decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered Docker incident evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design stdout and stderr logging plus driver, delivery, rotation, retention, metadata, backpressure, and secret-redaction policy.
Correlate time-bounded events with inspect state, exit cause, OOM, restart count, health output, mounts, networks, and effective configuration.
Interpret CPU, memory, network, block I/O, PIDs, cgroups, writable-layer size, image store, volume, and host pressure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Docker Logs Drivers Rotation · changed case · Quiz Docker Logs Drivers Rotation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz container exits only after revision 3; an operator restarts it and edits files with exec before collecting state. Reconstruct the first causal layer from inspect, events, logs, stats, and release identity.

Which action demonstrates Docker Logs Drivers Rotation without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Docker Logs Drivers Rotation · changed case · Quiz Docker Logs Drivers Rotation.
